title: Auto Review Pipeline Run Authorization
description: Human-approved, committed per-stage authorization artifact that gates entry into the auto_review_pipeline/v1 mode. The runner refuses to start (or continue) unless a schema-valid artifact of this shape exists and is not expired. Model claims of approval are never valid: 'authorized_by' must be human, 'approval_evidence_path' must point to the committed operator approval receipt, and 'approval_recorded_by' must identify the non-implementer bookkeeper that recorded it. v1 is serial-only: there is no authorization-side topology, allowed-adapter, review-provider, authorized flag, or high-end-dispatch field. The automatic loop is fixed to claude_glm/kimi/grok with Grok review-1 primary plus serial Kimi/Claude-GLM fallback by committed workflow/registry; GPT/Claude are never auto-dispatched. The stage-rework cap (3), the invalid-JSON retry limit (2), and per-adapter invocation timeouts are global workflow/registry invariants, not per-authorization fields. Frozen budgets retained here: 'max_model_calls' is a positive per-stage cap and 'max_auto_code_changes' is at most 2. 'expires_at' is required and nullable: null means there is no independent authorization-expiry timestamp (it is NOT unlimited authorization); a non-null ISO8601 value is enforced before every model invocation and git commit. A null 'expires_at' still leaves the run bound by call-count budgets, the rework ledger, operator stop, and stage gates. Any change to scope or budgets requires a new versioned artifact whose 'supersedes' points to the prior one. This artifact is never generated or edited by an implementer. Stale artifacts carrying a removed field (authorized, allowed_adapters, review_1_provider, auto_high_end_dispatch_allowed, scope.topology, budgets.wall_clock_seconds, budgets.max_stage_rework, budgets.invalid_json_max_attempts_per_model) fail closed via additionalProperties:false; v1 has not been accepted, merged, or piloted, so there is no compatibility-normalization path.

safe_required_repo_relative_path.description: Non-empty repo-relative path. Rejects absolute paths, parent-directory traversal ('..'), and CR, LF, tab, or any other control character.

safe_nullable_repo_relative_path.description: Nullable repo-relative path. null when not applicable; a non-null value must be a non-empty repo-relative path and is rejected if it is absolute, contains parent-directory traversal ('..'), or contains CR, LF, tab, or any other control character.

stage_branch.description: The stage branch this authorization is bound to, normally 'stage/<stage-id>'. The runner refuses a branch mismatch. No '^stage/' prefix is required here so an explicit user-approved branch exception recorded at intake remains schema-valid; the runner still requires exact equality with the current status/branch.

authorized_by.description: Only a human operator may authorize a run. Model-generated authorization is invalid. A committed, schema-valid, human-approved artifact existing and not being expired is the entry gate; there is no separate 'authorized' boolean field.

approval_evidence_path.description: Path to the committed operator approval receipt (e.g. auto-run-authorization-v1.approval.md) under the stage evidence directory. Safe repo-relative path; rejects absolute, parent-directory traversal, and CR/LF/control characters.

approval_recorded_by.description: Identity of the non-implementer bookkeeper that recorded the human approval into this artifact.

authorized_at.description: ISO8601 instant at which the human approval was recorded.

expires_at.description: Required and nullable. null means no independent authorization-expiry timestamp; it is NOT unlimited authorization and the run remains bound by call-count budgets, the rework ledger, operator stop, and stage gates. A non-null ISO8601 value must be later than the current time before every model invocation and git commit. A missing field is invalid.

task_ids.description: Frozen task ids this authorization covers. Must be non-empty and unique; an empty or duplicate task list is invalid. v1 review units are task-only (no tip or integration unit).

allowed_pathspecs.description: Frozen git pathspecs the automatic implementer/fix work is allowed to touch. The runner stages only these and fails on any other change.

forbidden_pathspecs.description: Frozen git pathspecs explicitly excluded even if an allowlist glob would otherwise match (e.g. product/runtime paths).

max_model_calls.description: Positive per-stage cap on model adapter calls. Each stage authorization freezes its own positive value; v1 has no global numeric default.

max_auto_code_changes.description: Maximum automatic code-changing retries (blocking-fix retries plus review-1 REWORK fixes) combined. Must be at most 2, preserving at least one review-2 repair opportunity within the global stage-rework cap of 3.

budgets.description: Per-authorization configurable caps. The stage-rework cap (3) and the invalid-JSON retry limit (2) are global workflow invariants and are NOT duplicated here; they are enforced from workflow/runtime constants and the top-level status ledger.

supersedes.description: Path to the prior authorization artifact this one supersedes, or null for the first version of this stage's authorization. When non-null it must be a safe repo-relative path; rejects absolute, parent-directory traversal, and CR/LF/control characters.
